keyTerms.Hadrian: Roman Emperor from 117 to 138 CE, known for consolidating the empire's borders rather than expanding them
keyTerms.milecastle: A fortified gateway built every Roman mile along Hadrian's Wall, housing a small garrison of 20-30 soldiers
keyTerms.Vindolanda tablets: Thin wooden writing tablets preserved in waterlogged soil, revealing everyday life in a Roman frontier garrison
keyTerms.auxiliary soldiers: Non-citizen troops recruited from across the Roman Empire who formed the bulk of the frontier garrison
keyTerms.Solway Firth: The inlet of the Irish Sea on the western end of Hadrian's Wall, marking the wall's terminus in Cumbria
